quiz_title: AL — Training Methods - Development of Flexibility and Coordination [EN]
description: Methods for developing flexibility and coordination, their role in motor learning, performance, and injury prevention.

questions.text: What is flexibility in training theory?
questions.explanation: Flexibility is the ability to move joints through their range of motion and to stretch muscles.

options.text: The ability of joint range of motion and muscle extensibility
The ability to sustain prolonged load

questions.explanation: Coordination is the ability to execute movements accurately, purposefully, and in a coordinated manner.

options.text: The ability to execute movements in a coordinated manner

questions.text: Which method is characteristic of static stretching?
questions.explanation: In static stretching, the athlete holds a stretched position for a defined period.
Dynamic stretching prepares muscles and joints for load through controlled movements.

options.text: Complete stillness for a long time

questions.text: When is it more appropriate to use dynamic stretching?
questions.explanation: Dynamic stretching is particularly useful during warm-up because it activates the movement system.

questions.text: When can static stretching be more beneficial?
questions.explanation: Static stretching is often useful at the end of training or in a dedicated mobility block to develop range of motion.

options.text: At the end of training or as dedicated mobility development
Directly before a sprint race start, held for a long time

questions.text: Developing flexibility can contribute to injury prevention.
questions.explanation: True. Adequate range of motion can support correct technical execution and reduce excessive tissue stress.

questions.text: Developing coordination is primarily related to improving nervous system regulation.
questions.explanation: True. Coordination is based on the motor control, sensory, and integrative functions of the nervous system.
Varied exercises involving decision-making situations and changes of direction best develop coordination.

questions.text: Why is variety important in coordination development?
questions.explanation: Varied stimuli force the nervous system to find new movement solutions, thereby improving motor control.

options.text: Because it develops adaptation to new movement situations
Because it always reduces training time
Because it completely replaces strength training
Because it eliminates the need for recovery

questions.text: Which statement correctly describes the relationship between flexibility and technique?
questions.explanation: Adequate flexibility enables technical elements to be executed through the required range of motion.

options.text: Technique is exclusively a matter of strength
Flexibility is only a theoretical concept

questions.text: Why can coordination deteriorate under the effect of fatigue?
questions.explanation: When fatigued, nervous system regulation, attention, and movement accuracy all deteriorate.

options.text: Because the precision of motor control decreases
Because coordination is independent of the nervous system

questions.text: Coordination exercises should always be performed after full fatigue.
questions.explanation: False. Tasks requiring precise motor learning and control should be performed in a fresh or less fatigued state.

questions.text: What is the most appropriate sequence within a training session when technical-coordinative development is also a goal?
questions.explanation: Coordinative and technical tasks are often more effective at the beginning of training, when the nervous system is less fatigued.

options.text: Coordinative-technical tasks at the beginning of training
The sequence is of no significance

questions.text: Which statement is correct about flexibility development?
questions.explanation: Flexibility development requires regularity, progressiveness, and method selection tailored to the demands of the sport.

options.text: It requires regular, progressive, and goal-oriented development
It can be established permanently with a single stretching session
It can only be developed after competition
It does not depend on the movement demands of the sport
